commit.message:
fix: replace stale tokens (--moon/--faint/--muted) with design system tokens

Pages and components were using var(--moon), var(--faint), var(--muted)
which are undefined in the current design system — causing invisible text.

Replacements:
  var(--moon)  → var(--cream)  (primary body text)
  var(--faint) → var(--mist)   (secondary/muted text)
  var(--muted) → var(--mist)   (same)

Files fixed:
  app/(app)/report/page.tsx
  app/(app)/settings/profile/page.tsx
  components/report/DashboardReport.tsx
  components/insights/TodaysGuidance.tsx
  components/insights/DailyInsightCard.tsx
  components/insights/LifePhaseIndicator.tsx

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add Makefile for running TLC model checker

Usage:
  make check MODEL=ThreePhaseCommitMajority  # Check specific model
  make check-all                             # Check all models
  make list                                  # List available models
  make clean                                 # Remove generated files

Requires tla2tools.jar at ~/lib/tla2tools.jar (or set TLA2TOOLS).

feat(sim): add simulation harness exercising all runtime use cases (#82) (#91)

* feat(sim): add simulation harness exercising all runtime use cases (#82)

Interactive simulation binary (lev-sim) that exercises:
- UC1: Declaration store with scope hierarchy, domains, capabilities,
  connectors, bindings, archetypes, views, validation, versioning
- UC2: Policy engine with 4 test cases (staging allow, prod deny,
  deny-by-default, capability-attached allow)
- UC3: Full execution pipeline through mock Claude Agent SDK and
  Motia adapters, including deny-by-default blocking
- UC4: Provenance log inspection, time-travel replay with execution
  timelines, and "why denied?" view
- UC5: Declaration versioning with SHA-256 hash chain integrity

https://claude.ai/code/session_012jNbgSYoZFfyZDLDw1BHRG

commit.message:
docs: add Anti-Pattern #13 (CJF-16) — false patch verification claims

Documents the folded-envelope patching incident (58 min lost):
- patch --dry-run --forward masks 3 failure modes
- cfl/verify-patches.sh provides ground truth (source+binary+runtime)
- Updated upstream-sync.prompt.md Step 6: verify-patches.sh mandatory
- Updated verification evidence examples in copilot-instructions.md
- Rule: build verification tooling on FIRST failure, not after repeats

Fix playback of C64U-hosted files (#122)

* fix: update FTP client to prefer LIST over MLSD for directory listings to prevent timeouts

fix: ensure binary uploads are sent as ArrayBuffer to avoid corruption for non-SID files

feat: implement native chunk reading for large HVSC archives to improve ingestion reliability

fix: add navigation guards to prevent loss of import progress during route changes

fix: optimize HVSC import throughput by avoiding unnecessary MD5 fallback during bulk imports

test: add unit tests for C64API binary upload handling and navigation guards

test: implement tests for songlength resolution logic to ensure correct behavior with and without MD5 fallback

* Update PLANS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(navigation): implement global navigation blocker and enhance navigation guards

- Added `GlobalNavigationBlocker` component to utilize `useNavigationGuardBlocker` for managing navigation transitions.
- Refactored `TabBar` component to simplify navigation handling by removing direct calls to `confirmNavigation`.
- Enhanced `navigationGuards` with `installNavigationBlocker` to manage blocked transitions and retries.
- Updated tests for navigation guards to cover new blocking behavior and retry logic.
- Improved `readArchiveBuffer` function in `hvscDownload` to validate chunk sizes and ensure complete reads from native platform.
- Added tests for handling large archives and error scenarios in `hvscDownload`.

* Add unit tests for executePlayPlan function in playbackRouter

- Implement tests to verify drive configuration for autoplaying disk images (d64, d71, d81).
- Mock necessary dependencies to isolate tests.
- Ensure correct order of API calls when powering on drives and setting modes.
- Validate behavior when Drive A is already ready, skipping unnecessary operations.

* fix: standardize formatting in playbackRouter and related test files

* Refactor BroadcastReceiver registration for Android 13+ compatibility

- Introduced BroadcastReceiverCompat to handle receiver registration with the appropriate flags for Android 13 (API level 33) and above.
- Updated BackgroundExecutionPlugin and DiagnosticsBridgePlugin to use the new registerPluginReceiver method for registering receivers.
- Enhanced unit tests for BackgroundExecutionPlugin and DiagnosticsBridgePlugin to verify receiver registration behavior on Android 13+.
- Added new tests to ensure proper handling of receiver registration failures.
- Updated various test descriptions for clarity and consistency.
- Added new tests for handling large playlist imports and ensuring lazy songlength lookups during bulk imports.
- Improved code readability and maintainability across multiple test files.

* Fix Android receiver compatibility and update proof

* Fix iOS settings subflow relaunch handling

* fix: correct indentation for "PLAY FILES" text in playback proof YAML

* Fix iOS Maestro settings subflow YAML

* Refactor README and enhance unit tests for navigation guards and HVSC ingestion

* fix: improve formatting of verification in BackgroundExecutionPluginTest

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Azure model requirements to README

Document required Azure Chat Completion and Azure Reasoning model deployments in prerequisites.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

cfl: add verify-patches.sh — ground-truth patch verification

3-phase verification replaces unreliable dry-run checks:
  Phase 1: Source-level — grep for patch code in iccDEV source
  Phase 2: Binary-level — nm/c++filt to confirm patched function signatures
  Phase 3: Runtime — timeout artifacts must complete in <2s budget

Addresses the folded-envelope problem: dry-run says 'applied' but that
masks context-shifted patches (CFL-031) and double-application risk.
Source grep + binary symbol check provides ground truth that dry-run cannot.

Also integrates --quick verification into build.sh post-build step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: .gcloudignore excluded .env.production from Cloud Build upload

Root cause chain:
1. .gcloudignore had '.env.*' — gcloud builds submit excluded .env.production
2. .env.production never reached Docker context
3. Next.js had no NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL at build time → 'supabaseUrl is required'

Fix: both .gcloudignore and .dockerignore now explicitly allow .env.production.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use resolved sendspin connection for MA server discovery

In auto-discovery mode (SENDSPIN_SERVER=auto), server_host is 'auto'
so the discover endpoint could not derive the MA API URL from it.
Now extracts the host from connected_server_url (the resolved address
from the live sendspin WebSocket connection) before falling back to
mDNS. Also deduplicated the logic into _ma_host_from_sendspin_clients()
helper used by both /api/ma/discover and /api/ma/login.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: account management — export/import bundles (M4)

Add `onecrawl account` CLI commands for portable account bundles:
- `account export <name>` — bundles auth_state + passkey vault entries
- `account import <file>` — restores auth_state + merges passkeys into vault
- `account list` — shows all saved account bundles
- `account show <name>` — displays bundle details
- `account delete <name>` — removes a bundle

AccountBundle format (v1):
  - auth_state: cookies + localStorage + sessionStorage + URL
  - passkeys: rp_id → PasskeyCredential[] mapping

Storage: ~/.onecrawl/accounts/{name}.json

All 574 tests pass, zero warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: zeroconf callback compatibility with kwargs-based API

Newer versions of zeroconf (0.131+) pass service state change
parameters as keyword arguments instead of positional. Our callback
parameter was named 'zc' but the library passes 'zeroconf', causing
TypeError. Fixed by using correct parameter name and adding **_kwargs
for forward compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refactor for readability, add diagrams and white paper

Major refactoring following programming principles (readability first,
small functions, DRY, meaningful names, no scripts):

Code changes:
- design.py: Break 220-line recurrent_mlp() into 10 focused functions
  (_validate_config, _define_kernels, _create_direct_fifos,
   _create_memtile_fifos, _make_worker_body, _column_activation_tap,
   _weight_tap, _create_runtime_sequence, etc.)
- test.py: Replace hardcoded paths with IRON_DIR env var + Path discovery;
  extract generate_test_data(), run_npu(), run_cpu(), check_correctness(),
  compute_metrics(), print_results() from monolithic benchmark()
- op.py: Add class/method docstrings, extract _artifact_name(), use class
  constants for compiler flags
- __init__.py: Improve module docstring, add assertion messages, rename
  block_r/block_c to block_rows/block_cols
- mlp_kernels.cc: Document SIMD width, alignment requirements, kernel
  contracts; rename size/count to num_elements

Documentation:
- docs/generate_figures.py: Creates 3 high-quality matplotlib diagrams
  (hardware architecture, recurrent MLP dataflow, performance scaling)
- docs/generate_whitepaper.py: Generates PDF white paper via weasyprint
  combining architecture explanation, diagrams, and benchmark results
- README.md: Replace ASCII art with diagram images, add project structure
  section, link to white paper

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: label-aware config fetching across exp, API, and website

exp service:
- Parameterize _load_azure_config(label) for per-label loading
- Add per-label cache (_config_by_label) with stale-check refresh
- Add get_config_for_label(label) — falls back to get_config() in local mode
- /api/v1/config accepts optional label query param

API (.NET):
- IConfigProxyClient.GetConfigValueAsync gains optional label parameter
- ConfigProxyClient appends &label= to URI when label is provided
- Startup and ConfigRefreshHostedService pass label='PRODUCTION'

Website (TypeScript):
- Derive CONFIG_LABEL from SITE_ENV (DEVELOPMENT or PRODUCTION)
- Append &label= to every config fetch

Tests:
- 2 new ConfigProxyClient tests (WithLabel, NullLabel)
- ConfigRefreshHostedService fake updated for new interface

Result: API always gets PRODUCTION config, dev website gets DEVELOPMENT,
prod website gets PRODUCTION.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES support for Mock Cluster (#2099)

### Issue

#2070 

### Description

Add support for TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES when creating mock and simulation
clusters as well. So far, target_devices were taking just the chips
specified in that set, not the ones from the boards where target chips
are. After this PR, user can use both TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES and
target_device_ids for creating mock and simulation cluster. When all
users transition to TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES, we could remove the support for
that.

### List of the changes

- Support TT_VISIBLE_DEVICES for mock and simulation cluster
- Refactor logic around creating cluster descriptor
- Take the chips from the same board where target devices are

### Testing
CI

### API Changes
/

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Ridvan Song <56486612+Riddy21@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Riddy21 <rsong@g14glx03.tenstorrent.net>

commit.message:
fix: use statusRef in search debounce to avoid stale closure

The search debounce captured status from its mount-time closure,
so if the user changed the status filter within 300ms of page load
the debounce would fire with the stale 'all' value, overwriting the
correct filtered results. Fix by mirroring the existing searchRef
pattern for status.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

.NET: Skip Azure Persistent (V1) flaky  CodeInterpreter integration tests (#4583)

* Skip flaky CodeInterpreter integration tests in CI

The CreateAgent_CreatesAgentWithCodeInterpreter tests fail intermittently
because the Azure AI Code Interpreter service sometimes fails to read/execute
uploaded Python files. This causes all 4 integration test jobs to fail
consistently across both platforms (ubuntu/windows) and TFMs (net10.0/net472).

Mark both test variants with Skip to match the convention used by other
flaky tests in the suite (e.g., AzureAIAgentsPersistentStructuredOutputRunTests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: outfit archetypes, one-piece/coord-set support, new enums & fields

## Summary
Implements the full archetype-based outfit system as specified:

### Part 1 — app_enums.dart
- Add `ClothingCategory.onePiece` ('One Piece')
- Add `ClothingSubcategory` enum (tops, bottoms, one-piece, shoes, outerwear)
- Add `ShoeFormality` enum (formal, casual, sporty)
- Add `OutfitArchetype` enum (separates, onePiece, onePieceLayered, coordSet, smartCasual)

### Part 2 — clothing_item_model.dart
- Add `subcategory`, `shoeFormality`, `setId`, `isOnePiece`, `replacesTop`,
  `replacesBottom` fields with safe defaults (backward-compatible)

### Part 3 — app_database.dart + app_constants.dart
- Bump schema version 2 → 3
- Add migration for new ClothingItems columns and Outfits.archetype/onePieceId

### Part 4 — archetype_engine.dart (NEW)
- `OutfitCombination`, `CoordSet`, `ArchetypeEngine`
- Detects available archetypes; generates season-filtered combinations

### Part 5 — outfit_scoring_engine.dart
- Delegates to `ArchetypeEngine`; adds shoe-pairing bonus and coord-set bonus
- Existing totalCombinationsEvaluated formula preserved (backward compat)

### Part 6 — outfit_model.dart
- Add `onePieceId` (nullable String) and `archetype` (OutfitArchetype)

### Part 7 — tagging_form.dart + add_item_providers.dart
- Dynamic form: subcategory selector, shoe formality, co-ord set section
- `TaggingFormState` extended with new fields; `isValid` updated

### Part 8 — clothing_item_card.dart
- Visual badges: ONE PIECE overlay, Co-ord chip, shoe formality chip

### Part 9 — filter_chip_bar.dart
- One Piece filter chip in correct position (Top, Bottom, One Piece, Shoes, Outerwear)

### Part 10 — outfit_card.dart
- Archetype label chip; coord-set link indicator between paired items

### Part 11 — add_item_usecase.dart
- `AddItemInput` extended with subcategory/shoeFormality/setId/isCoordPiece

### Part 12 — local_clothing_datasource.dart + clothing_repository.dart
- New methods: `getOnePieceItems`, `getItemsBySetId`, `getCoordSets`,
  `linkCoordSet`, `unlinkCoordSet`
- Row mapping updated for all new columns

### Part 13 — Tests
- `home_screen_test.dart`: fix saveAll stub, add todaysOutfitsProvider override,
  update icon/text to match current home_screen implementation
- `archetype_engine_test.dart` (NEW): 19 tests covering archetype detection,
  combination generation, scoring bonuses, and backward compat
- `repository_providers.dart`: remove unnecessary LocalOutfitDatasource cast
  in todaysOutfitsProvider (was breaking test setup)

All 121 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update page metadata: title Novaro-Build, description Studyplanner TUM

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
